title: avena cultiformis
intro: Avena cultiformis, also known as wild oat or oat grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the wheat family and is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Avena cultiformis has been found to provide several health benefits, including relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Avena cultiformis, such as teas and tinctures, are commonly used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
